title: Blyth Green (1) Wellington Memorial
crew_summary: 4 killed including Plt Off. Frank Reginald Bedford

external_narratives.summary: The Nottinghamshire Aviation Memorials guide documents the memorial at Blyth village green for Wellington HE818 of 18 OTU (RAF Worksop), which crashed approximately 200 metres from the memorial site on 7 March 1944.
Aviation forum discussion researching the Wellington HE818 crash from 18 OTU at Worksop, with contributors sharing details of the crew and circumstances of the crash near Blyth.
Aviation Archaeology records Wellington HE818 in its 1944 military crashes listing for the south-west Midlands region, confirming the crash near Blyth on 7 March 1944.
key_details: Listed in Midlands military crash database

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: OSM node 280070874 Halifax Bomber Memorial (53.0918575 -1.0403268 near Farnsfield)

INACCURATE. Previous coords (53.076465 -0.879203) placed pin near Staythorpe/Newark. OSM has a named Halifax Bomber Memorial ~13km west at Farnsfield. NOTE: Possible coordinates swap — OSM Wellington crash memorial is at 53.190 -0.914 (attributed to other Blyth Green site). The two Blyth Green entries may have swapped coordinates. Requires physical site verification to confirm which OSM node corresponds to HE818 Wellington vs NA581 Halifax.
